[2024-04-11 04:39] pikachumemes. (Pikachu Memes): a lot of the big red units are "first player to get them wins", that's literally how they are designed. This is true of most strong prismata attackers. 
I notice in some of the replays you are attempting to outvalue big red--this is not a good idea, it's basically doomed to lose. A side-effect of this is a lot of games where you respond to double animus with animus (game 10 is a good example of this); this is not a good idea as you're typically not spending RR and tarsier is terrible in high inflation. There's a good video at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=puZ7kcMDZYA showing how bad animus is as a response to double animus. This is because getting an animus is typically a signal you're trying to win a long, drawn-out game by getting units like Tarsiers that are good long-term, but you're never going to outvalue double animus.
Overall, the response to double animus should be "how do I get attack ASAP, long term consequences be damned";  I'm not sure I'm seeing this kind of mentality in a lot of your replays (games 6, 10, and 11 come to mind)

[2024-04-11 07:05] pikachumemes. (Pikachu Memes): That's kind of true but there were def situations where you could have built better:
Game 1 imo is just a misreading of the set and I'm pretty sure fastimus handiliy beats 3rd engineer even if you take tatsu out here; 
Game 2 is not a strong enough reaction to Animus + centrifudge; I think you have to go CCA + zeno guardian on t6 (after he buys fudge) and click doubly iceblade + venge every turn and stop defending at all. Turns out doomed drones suck against double animus
Game 3 is IMO just a set misread; should be fastimus here
Game 4 is actually decided by shadowfang singlehandedly; this is imo the first decent showing of the complaint (although as i said i think you missed an opening to be the first to AA)
Game 5 for example going DDCC instead of DDDB is probably better even without Lucina because it happens to set up Fabricator so this is just a straight up misplay
Game 6 is not a strong enough reaction to AA--IMO the critical mistake here was getting a forge and defending instead of going YOLO with gauss cannons (which by analysis BARELY wins with correct play; you have to kill 2 wilds on turn 7)
Game 7 idk what happened
Game 8 im pretty sure is a fastimus angle; seems like a set misread (Although to be fair, it is a fastimus angle due to big red existing)
Game 9 im pretty sure 3rd engi is just wrong even without ampo in the set. I would be very surprised if you removed ampo if 3rd engi beat natural conduit here
Game 10 possibly a legitimate complaint against shadowfang's existence, need to analyze more
Game 11 going deadeye in a lancetooth set is a criminal offense
Game 12 After analysis, im sure is lost by a bad response to AA
Game 13 I'm pretty sure is lost by going 3rd engineer, just seems like a bad set read

Overall I'm not conviced these replays (other than 10/4) are lost specifcally due to "big red problems" as opposed to regular prismata things

[2024-04-13 19:57] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So prolly should be reserved for like a special tournament format

[2024-04-13 20:49] aa01blue: wait isn't that always bad

[2024-04-13 20:50] aa01blue: but in practice nobody buys grimbotches to soak them in 1 turn
[2024-04-13 20:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): and it is held immediately
[2024-04-13 20:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): if you are protecting your big absorber
[2024-04-13 20:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): or your squishy backline
[2024-04-13 20:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but you won't soak these sier->botches immediately either
[2024-04-13 20:51] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): you will convert them in advance
[2024-04-13 20:51] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): for when you need to soak them later
[2024-04-13 20:51] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but I see your point
[2024-04-13 20:51] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): it likely needs to generate some more value
[2024-04-13 20:51] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): more than just sier->botch
[2024-04-13 20:51] aa01blue: i mean, I struggle to see an inflation where botch is better than bt1 tarsier
[2024-04-13 20:53] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): what if it's build-time, and clicking it converts all your siers
[2024-04-13 20:53] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): like say the unit is bt2 or something
[2024-04-13 20:53] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): you can click it whenever
[2024-04-13 20:53] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): to convert all your siers
[2024-04-13 20:54] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I guess maybe it's too simple-minded then
[2024-04-13 20:54] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): in that you literally only use it for big soak needs
[2024-04-13 20:54] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): i.e. when game ending breach is imminent
[2024-04-13 20:55] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): either once drone count is low and attack is high, or when a big burst is incoming
[2024-04-13 20:56] aa01blue: I feel like it would probably involve a lot of inflation calcs to balance
